content.text: I need a way to list all potential namespace dictionaries where objects created by a user in an IPython session might currently be stored.

This function is designed for introspection, gathering every known location within the IPython environment that references namespaces. It compiles these locations into a single list of dictionary references.

```python
def all_ns_refs(self):
        """Get a list of references to all the namespace dictionaries in which
        IPython might store a user-created object.
        
        Note that this does not include the displayhook, which also caches
        objects from the output."""
        return [self.user_ns, self.user_global_ns,
                self._user_main_module.__dict__] + self._main_ns_cache.values()
```

The returned list provides direct access to all current namespace dict objects used by IPython while running the session.
If I use this function in an interactive shell, will it include variables defined inside functions or class methods?
No, this function only tracks top-level and globally accessible namespaces. Variables created within scopes like local functions or classes are not captured by these direct references.
That makes sense; I just wanted to check the scope boundaries.
